_comment: REPRESENTATIVE captured-geometry fixture (NOT synthetic single-scale). THE STRADDLE CASE. Dual monitor: primary A is physical x[0,2560) at 150% scale and hosts the main window; secondary B is physical x[2560,4480) at 100% scale to the right. The main window sits near A's right edge so the anchor overlay crosses the 2560 boundary into B. Critical point: the physical rect is built using ONLY A's scale (1.5) applied to the anchor offset; the window position came from the OS already physical, so we NEVER apply B's 100% scale to the half on B. The rect stays correct and the OS renders each half at its own DPI.
description: dual monitor straddle 150%/100% — overlay crosses the DPI boundary
